title: Data Analyst (entry level)
description:
Title: Data Analyst (entry level)

Duration: FTE/perm

Location: onsite in NYC

Salary: 50-70k DOE




This is a great opportunity for a recent graduate or early-career professional (0–3 years experience) who wants to build a career in data, analytics, and business intelligence. You’ll gain hands-on experience working with modern data tools like SQL, Python, Power BI, Snowflake, and Databricks, while supporting real business decisions across the organization.




You’ll work closely with experienced analysts, engineers, and business stakeholders, learning how to turn raw data into dashboards, insights, and actionable recommendations that drive impact.




What You’ll Do

Reporting & Dashboards

Build and maintain reports and dashboards in Power BISupport KPI tracking and business performance reportingAssist with ad hoc analysis for business teamsData Analysis

Work with sales, customer, financial, and operational dataIdentify trends, patterns, and insights from dataHelp translate findings into simple, clear business insightsData & Pipeline Support

Assist with ETL/ELT data pipelines and data integrationSupport data quality checks and troubleshooting issuesHelp prepare and structure data for reporting and analyticsCloud Data Platforms

Query datasets using SQL in Snowflake and/or DatabricksSupport data preparation for dashboards and analysisHelp improve data accessibility and reporting efficiencyAI & Innovation Exposure

Support early-stage AI and automation initiativesParticipate in proof-of-concept analytics and AI projectsLearn how AI is used to improve reporting and decision-makingCollaboration & Learning

Work with business teams to understand reporting needsPartner with senior analysts on analytics initiativesDocument data definitions and reporting processesLearn best practices in analytics, BI, and data engineering


What We’re Looking For

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Business, or related field0–2 years of experience (internships, co-ops, or projects welcome)Strong interest in data, analytics, and problem-solvingBasic SQL knowledge (or willingness to learn quickly)Familiarity with Excel and/or Power BI is a plusExposure to Python is a plus but not requiredStrong communication skills and eagerness to learnNice to Have (But Not Required)

Internship or project experience in analytics or BIExposure to Snowflake, Databricks, or cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)Familiarity with dashboards or reporting toolsInterest in AI, automation, or modern data tools
reasoning: Michael is an exceptionally strong candidate, aligning perfectly with the 'recent graduate/early-career professional' profile and possessing strong SQL, Python, and data visualization skills demonstrated through multiple robust projects. His experience with AWS and AI integration in projects further enhances his appeal, showing a proactive interest in modern data tools beyond the basic requirements. While specific Power BI, Snowflake, or Databricks experience isn't listed, his comprehensive foundational skills and eagerness to learn make him highly trainable for these tools.

title: Entry Level/No Experience Data Entry
description:
Turn your feedback into real impact and rewards. Participate in online or in-person focus groups, surveys, and product testing sessions to help brands improve their products and services. Flexible scheduling allows you to earn income on your own terms from home or in-person.

 Role and Responsibilities:

  Take part in paid research studies, including focus groups and surveys. Provide honest, thoughtful feedback through written or verbal responses. Test products or services when required and share your experiences accurately. Follow project guidelines and contribute meaningfully to each study.  Required Skills and Experience:

  Must be a U.S. resident aged 18 or older. Access to a reliable device with a camera (computer, tablet, or smartphone) and internet. Ability to understand and follow both written and verbal instructions. No prior research or data entry experience required.  Preferred Qualifications:

  Interest in consumer products, market research, and providing feedback. Reliability and consistency in completing assigned research tasks. Comfortable expressing opinions clearly in both individual and group settings.  Compensation and Earnings:

  Earn between $75 and $750 per assignment depending on study length and complexity. Flexible schedule-participate full-time, part-time, or occasionally. Opportunities to test and review new products before public release. Payments made via PayPal, direct bank transfer, or gift cards.  About Apex Focus Group:

 Apex Focus Group connects participants with safe, legitimate research opportunities across the U.S. It’s a flexible platform for individuals to share opinions, influence products, and earn extra income from home or in-person.
reasoning: Michael is exceptionally well-suited for this role, possessing strong communication, critical thinking, and organizational skills, which are ideal for providing thoughtful feedback in research studies. His prior experience as a Data Entry Specialist, coupled with advanced data analysis projects, demonstrates a high capacity for precision and adherence to instructions. Although overqualified for an "entry-level/no experience" role, he meets and significantly exceeds all specified requirements and preferred qualifications.

title: Entry-Level Remote Data Analyst at cewit New York, NY
description:
Job Overview
We are seeking a detailoriented and analytical EntryLevel Data Analyst to join our remote team. This role is an excellent opportunity for individuals looking to build a career in data analysis with ample opportunities for growth and professional development. You will be responsible for collecting analyzing and interpreting data to provide insights that support business decisions. No prior experience is necessary as we will provide comprehensive training for the right candidate.


Key Responsibilities Collect clean and organize data from various sources to ensure data accuracy and completeness. Perform data analysis using statistical methods and software tools to uncover trends patterns and insights. Create visual reports and dashboards to communicate findings clearly to stakeholders.


Assist in the development of data models and predictive analytics to support decisionmaking processes. Collaborate with crossfunctional teams (such as marketing sales and operations) to understand data needs and provide relevant insights. Continuously monitor data quality and identify areas for improvement in data collection and analysis processes.


Maintain confidentiality of sensitive company data and adhere to data protection regulations.


Qualifications Bachelors degree in a related field (e.g. Data Science Statistics Mathematics Computer Science Economics or a similar discipline) is preferred but not required. Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.


Basic knowledge of statistical analysis and data visualization tools (such as Excel Power BI or Tableau) is a plus. Familiarity with SQL or other database languages is preferred but not mandatory. Strong problemsolving skills and ability to think critically.


Excellent communication skills both written and verbal. Selfmotivated and able to work independently in a remote setting. Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.


Experience No prior work experience is required for this entrylev
reasoning: The candidate is an excellent fit, possessing strong hands-on experience in data cleaning, SQL, Python, and data visualization demonstrated through robust projects involving large datasets and complex database design. Their Computer Science degree and Google Data Analytics certificate directly align with and exceed the preferred qualifications, making them highly suitable for this entry-level role despite the stated "no prior experience necessary".

title: Entry Level Data Scientist/Analyst/Java full stack developer-remote
description:
Getting Rejections? Recruiters Ghosting You? Build a Stronger Profile!

Applied to 500+ Jobs and Still No Interviews? Your Resume Isn't Getting Past ATS! Get offers with a process!

Every job posting attracts hundreds or thousands of applicants, making it nearly impossible to stand out. Most job seekers blame their resume when they don't get interviews, but the real issue is positioning. A degree gives you a foundation, but employers want more — they want proof you can apply your knowledge in realworld scenarios. Employers want candidates who demonstrate practical skills, confidence, and readiness.

SynergisticIT gives you the tools to rise above the noise. Getting hired in tech isn't just about knowing how to code — it's about proving you can deliver value from day one. Getting interviews but not converting them into offers is one of the most frustrating stages of a tech job search. It's also one of the most fixable—because interview performance is rarely about intelligence. It's usually about preparation structure, repetition, communication clarity, and knowing what interviewers actually test.

Many candidates learn coding, but they don't learn how to present their skills under pressure. SynergisticIT is designed for candidates who want to stop guessing and start improving with a clear framework.

Since 2010, SynergisticIT has helped thousands of candidates land full-time jobs at tech leaders and enterprise employers—companies such as Google, Apple, PayPal, Visa, Western Union, Wells Fargo, Client, Walmart Labs, Client, Banking, Client, Client, and many more—with offers often ranging from $95,000 to $154,000 depending on role and skill depth. The focus is: build job-ready ability + interview confidence + hiring alignment so you can close the deal when opportunities appear.

Why do people fail interviews after doing CS or online courses? Typically it's one (or several) of these gaps:

Weak fundamentals (you know syntax, but not the "why”)Poor project explanation (you built something, but can't defend design decisions)Shallow system understanding (APIs, DB design, CI/CD, cloud basics are fuzzy)No repetition under pressure (whiteboard/online assessments feel unfamiliar)Lack of structured mock interview practice

SynergisticIT addresses these gaps by treating interviews as a skill you work on—like a sport. You don't just watch videos; you practice real drills. We emphasize on real interview patterns: coding questions, debugging, project walkthroughs, behavioral responses, and the ability to speak clearly about your work.

What kinds of roles are being targeted?

Instead of chasing every shiny trend, JOPP focuses on roles employers repeatedly hire for: Java full stack, software programming, Python/Java development, DevOps, data analyst, data engineer, data scientist, and ML/AI engineer. In other words, the program builds candidates across Java / Full Stack / DevOps and Data Analytics / Data Engineering / Data Science / Machine Learning / AI—because companies hire teams, not single-skill candidates.

Ideal Candidates Who Benefit From Interview-focused Help

This includes: recent grads with limited experience, laid-off professionals re-entering the market, career changers, candidates with gaps, experienced applicants who can't convert interviews, and F1/OPT candidates needing a stable path. SynergisticIT also supports candidates with guidance around STEM extension and offers process support relating to H-1B/Green Card filing once employed (as applicable through employers and standard immigration processes).

Please read our blogs

Why do Tech Companies not Hire recent Computer Science Graduates | SynergisticIT

What Recruiters Look for in Junior Developers | SynergisticIT

Software engineering or Data Science as a career?

If you're already getting interviews, you're closer than you think. Now it's time to train like you mean it—and turn interviews into offers.

If you want to explore, here are the key links:

Event videos (OCW, JavaOne, Gartner):USA Today featureClient JOPP: Job Placement ProgramContact Form (Get Started): https://www.synergisticit.com/contact-us/

Please note: Resume databases are shared with clients and interested clients will reach out directly if they find a qualified candidate for their req.

Resume submissions may be shared with our JOPP team database also. Please unsubscribe if contacted or if you don't want to be contacted please don't submit your resume.
reasoning: The candidate is a strong fit for the 'Entry Level Data Scientist/Analyst' aspect, possessing a CS degree, a Data Analytics certificate, and robust projects showcasing SQL, Python, database design on AWS, and data visualization. Their profile as a recent CS grad seeking a first technical role aligns perfectly with SynergisticIT's target audience of candidates needing interview training and placement assistance, even with listed Java skills for full stack roles.

title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)
description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)




Role Description

Support data-driven decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from various business and operational sources. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with actionable insights into business performance, trends, and key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze data to identify patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear and meaningful reports. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance monitoring, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, performance measurement, and process improvement principles is a plus.

title: Entry-Level Data Analyst
description:
Our client is looking for an enthusiastic and detail-oriented Entry-Level Data Analyst to join their team in New York City, New York, US  . This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates to kick-start their career in a fast-paced and innovative environment. As an Entry-Level Data Analyst, you will assist in collecting, cleaning, and analyzing large datasets to provide actionable insights and support business decision-making. You will work closely with experienced analysts and data scientists, gaining hands-on experience with various data analysis tools and methodologies.

Key Responsibilities:
Assist in gathering data from various sources and ensuring data accuracy and completeness.  Perform data cleaning and pre-processing to prepare datasets for analysis.  Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies.  Generate reports and visualizations to communicate findings to stakeholders.  Support senior analysts in developing and implementing data-driven solutions.  Learn and apply statistical methods and data mining techniques.  Assist in the maintenance of databases and data management systems.  Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs and provide analytical support.  Contribute to the documentation of data processes and analytical methodologies.  Participate in team meetings and contribute to brainstorming sessions for new analytical approaches.    
Qualifications:
Recent graduate with a Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field.  Strong understanding of basic statistical concepts and data analysis principles.  Familiarity with data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, and Python or R is a plus.  Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a keen attention to detail.  Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.  Eagerness to learn and adapt to new technologies and methodologies.  Ability to work independently and as part of a team.    This internship offers a valuable learning experience, providing exposure to real-world data challenges and the opportunity to develop critical analytical skills. We are committed to fostering a supportive environment where interns can grow and contribute meaningfully. Come and be part of a team that values data-driven insights.

title: Entry Level Data Scientist/Analyst/Java full stack developer
description:
1000's of Job Applications and No Interviews or Job offers? Let's get you hired.

If you've been unemployed for over 3-6+ months, you're not alone—and you're not out of options. The tech industry is competitive, and having a career gap can make it harder to get callbacks. But at SynergisticIT, we specialize in helping candidates like you regain momentum, rebuild confidence, and get hired.

Why You're Being Overlooked—and How We Can Help

Many employers today don't just want a degree—they're looking for job-ready developers with hands-on experience, relevant certifications, and up-to-date skills in today's tech stack. Unfortunately, most CS grads don't get that in college.

Since 2010, we've helped thousands of candidates land full-time jobs at tech leaders like Google, Apple, PayPal, Visa, Western Union, Wells Fargo, Client, Paypal, Banking, Wayfair, Client, Client and hundreds more with Job offers of $95k to $154k. Synergisticit focuses on closing the gap between your tech skills and what employers want now.

Currently, We are looking for entry-level software programmers, Java Full stack developers, Python/Java developers, Data analysts/Data Engineers/ Data Scientists, Machine Learning engineers for full time positions with clients.

We Focus on Java /Full stack/Devops and Data Science /Data Engineers/Data analysts/BI Analysts/ Machine learning/AI candidates

Entry Level Business Management




We are looking to train an entry level sales manager from the ground up to act as the first point of contact with new customers, answer their queries and increase client satisfaction.




If you enjoy coming up with effective solutions and working toward achieving goals, this job is right for you. You will use your communication skills to identify and address clients’ needs while representing our company in a positive way. Previous sales experience will be an advantage.




Ultimately, you will contribute to building profitable, long-term relationships with our clients to reach our business objectives.
